lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਬਦਿ ਮਰੈ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨੀਐ ਸਾਚੇ ਸਾਚੀ ਸੋਇ ॥੩੩॥
lines.translit: naanak sabad marai man maaneeai saache saachee soe |33|
lines.english: O Nanak, when someone dies in the Word of the Shabad, the mind is pleased and appeased. True is the reputation of those who are true. ||33||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਹੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਦੁਤਰੁ ਤਰਿਆ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa mohu dukh saagar hai bikh dutar tariaa na jaae |
lines.english: Emotional attachment to Maya is a treacherous ocean of pain and poison, which cannot be crossed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰਾ ਮੇਰਾ ਕਰਦੇ ਪਚਿ ਮੁਏ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਰਤ ਵਿਹਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: meraa meraa karade pach mue haumai karat vihaae |
lines.english: Screaming, "Mine, mine!", they rot and die; they pass their lives in egotism.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਾ ਉਰਵਾਰੁ ਨ ਪਾਰੁ ਹੈ ਅਧ ਵਿਚਿ ਰਹੇ ਲਪਟਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukhaa uravaar na paar hai adh vich rahe lapattaae |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs are in limbo, neither on this side, nor the other; they are stuck in the middle.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਧੁਰਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਸੁ ਕਮਾਵਣਾ ਕਰਣਾ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jo dhur likhiaa su kamaavanaa karanaa kachhoo na jaae |
lines.english: They act as they are pre-destined; they cannot do anything else.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਰਤਨੁ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਸਭੁ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਸੁਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: guramatee giaan ratan man vasai sabh dekhiaa braham subhaae |
lines.english: Following the Guru's Teachings, the jewel of spiritual wisdom abides in the mind, and then God is easily seen in all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਬੋਹਿਥੈ ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ਚੜੈ ਤੇ ਭਉਜਲਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਲੰਘਾਇ ॥੩੪॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur bohithai vaddabhaagee charrai te bhaujal paar langhaae |34|
lines.english: O Nanak, the very fortunate ones embark on the Boat of the True Guru; they are carried across the terrifying world-ocean. ||34||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਾਤਾ ਕੋ ਨਹੀ ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਦੇਇ ਆਧਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur daataa ko nahee jo har naam dee aadhaar |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, there is no giver who can bestow the Support of the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਨਾਉ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੈ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur kirapaa te naau man vasai sadaa rahai ur dhaar |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the Name comes to dwell in the mind; keep it enshrined in your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਹਰਿ ਅਪਨੀ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਧਾਰਿ ॥੩੫॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh paaeeai har apanee kirapaa dhaar |35|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh finds the Lord, when He showers His Mercy. ||35||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸਨਾ ਬੁਝੈ ਤਿਪਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਨਾਇ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: tisanaa bujhai tipat hoe har kai naae piaar |
lines.english: The fire of desire is extinguished, and one finds satisfaction, through the Love of the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਖੇ ਸੇ ਉਬਰੇ ਸਬਦਿ ਰਹੇ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: har rakhe se ubare sabad rahe liv laae |
lines.english: Those who are protected by the Lord are saved; they remain lovingly attuned to the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਬਦੈ ਜਗਤੁ ਬਰਲਿਆ ਕਹਣਾ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin sabadai jagat baraliaa kahanaa kachhoo na jaae |
lines.english: Without the Shabad, the world is so insane, that it cannot even be described.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਰਤਾ ਸਭ ਕਿਛੁ ਜਾਣਦਾ ਜਿਨਿ ਰਖੀ ਬਣਤ ਬਣਾਇ ॥੩੬॥
lines.translit: naanak karataa sabh kichh jaanadaa jin rakhee banat banaae |36|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Creator who made this making knows everything. ||36||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਮ ਜਗ ਸਭਿ ਤੀਰਥਾ ਪੜੑਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਥਕੇ ਪੁਰਾਣ ॥
lines.translit: hom jag sabh teerathaa parra panddit thake puraan |
lines.english: The Pandits, the religious scholars, have grown weary of making fire-offerings and sacrifices, making pilgrimages to all the sacred shrines, and reading the Puraanas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਈ ਵਿਚਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਆਵਣੁ ਜਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: bikh maaeaa mohu na mittee vich haumai aavan jaan |
lines.english: But they cannot get rid of the poison of emotional attachment to Maya; they continue to come and go in egotism.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸੇਵਿਆ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਸਦ ਕੁਰਬਾਣੁ ॥੩੭॥
lines.translit: jinaa har har prabh seviaa jan naanak sad kurabaan |37|
lines.english: Servant Nanak is forever a sacrifice to those who serve their Lord God. ||37||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਮਲੁ ਉਤਰੀ ਹਰਿ ਜਪਿਆ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਸੁਜਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai mal utaree har japiaa purakh sujaan |
lines.english: Meeting with the True Guru, one's filth is washed off, meditating on the Lord, the Primal Being, the All-knowing One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਅਸਥਿਰੁ ਨਾ ਥੀਐ ਮਰਿ ਬਿਨਸਿ ਜਾਇ ਖਿਨ ਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh asathir naa theeai mar binas jaae khin vaar |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs do not become steady and stable; they die and are gone in an instant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਬਹੁ ਚਿਤਵਦੇ ਬਹੁ ਆਸਾ ਲੋਭੁ ਵਿਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa mohu bahu chitavade bahu aasaa lobh vikaar |
lines.english: Mortals give great thought to Maya and emotional attachment; they harbor great hopes, in greed and corruption.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ਜਪਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਬਦੁ ਵੀਚਾਰ ॥੩੮॥
lines.translit: har naamaa jap sukh paaeaa jan naanak sabad veechaar |38|
lines.english: Chanting the Name of the Lord, they find peace; servant Nanak contemplates the Word of the Shabad. ||38||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਭਾਗੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਤਜੈ ਵਿਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: vaddabhaag hovai satigur milai haumai tajai vikaar |
lines.english: Only those who are blessed with great good fortune meet the True Guru, and leave behind their egotism and corruption.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਨਾਮਿ ਨ ਲਗੈ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: bin satigur bhagat na hovee naam na lagai piaar |
lines.english: Without the True Guru, there is no devotional worship, and no love of the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਰਾਧਿਆ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਹੇਤਿ ਪਿਆਰਿ ॥੩੯॥
lines.translit: jan naanak naam araadhiaa gur kai het piaar |39|
lines.english: Servant Nanak worships and adores the Naam, with love and affection for the Guru. ||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਭੀ ਕਾ ਵੇਸਾਹੁ ਨ ਕੀਜੈ ਜੇ ਕਾ ਪਾਰਿ ਵਸਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: lobhee kaa vesaahu na keejai je kaa paar vasaae |
lines.english: Do not trust greedy people, if you can avoid doing so.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਿ ਕਾਲਿ ਤਿਥੈ ਧੁਹੈ ਜਿਥੈ ਹਥੁ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ant kaal tithai dhuhai jithai hath na paae |
lines.english: At the very last moment, they will deceive you there, where no one will be able to lend a helping hand.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਹ ਕਾਲੇ ਤਿਨੑ ਲੋਭੀਆਂ ਜਾਸਨਿ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: muh kaale tina lobheean jaasan janam gavaae |
lines.english: Black are the faces of those greedy people; they lose their lives, and leave in disgrace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਸੇਤੀ ਸੰਗੁ ਕਰੇ ਮੁਹਿ ਕਾਲਖ ਦਾਗੁ ਲਗਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh setee sang kare muhi kaalakh daag lagaae |
lines.english: Whoever associates with the self-willed manmukhs, will have his face blackened and dirtied.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਸੰਗਤਿ ਹਰਿ ਮੇਲਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਸੈ ਮਨਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: satasangat har mel prabh har naam vasai man aae |
lines.english: O Lord, let me join the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation; may the Name of the Lord God abide in my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਸੁ ਮੇਟਣਾ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dhur har prabh karatai likhiaa su mettanaa na jaae |
lines.english: Whatever is pre-destined by the Lord God Creator, cannot be erased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਕੀ ਮਲੁ ਉਤਰੈ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ॥੪੦॥
lines.translit: janam maran kee mal utarai jan naanak har gun gaae |40|
lines.english: The filth and pollution of birth and death is washed away, O servant Nanak, singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||40||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਸਭੁ ਤਿਸ ਦਾ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲਿ ਕਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jeeo pindd sabh tis daa pratipaal kare har raae |
lines.english: Body and soul are all His. The Sovereign Lord King cherishes all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੁਗਲ ਨਿੰਦਕ ਭੁਖੇ ਰੁਲਿ ਮੁਏ ਏਨਾ ਹਥੁ ਨ ਕਿਥਾਊ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: chugal nindak bhukhe rul mue enaa hath na kithaaoo paae |
lines.english: The gossipers and slanderers shall remain hungry and die, rolling in the dust; their hands cannot reach anywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੇਤਿ ਸਰੀਰਿ ਜੋ ਬੀਜੀਐ ਸੋ ਅੰਤਿ ਖਲੋਆ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: khet sareer jo beejeeai so ant khaloaa aae |
lines.english: Whatever is planted in the farm of the body, shall come and stand before them in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਪਾਖੰਡ ਸਭ ਕਰਮ ਕਰਹਿ ਮਨਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਕਪਟੁ ਕਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: baahar paakhandd sabh karam kareh man hiradai kapatt kamaae |
lines.english: Outwardly, they do all the proper deeds, but they are hypocrites; in their minds and hearts, they practice deception and fraud.
